Valid: ' + Object.keys(SPLASH_PRESETS).join(', '));\n  1939\t  }\n  1940\t\n  1941\t  markCanvasActive();\n  1942\t  setActiveRectFull();\n  1943\t\n  1944\t  // -- 1. Lift deposited \u2192 suspended, saturate wet.\n  1945\t  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\n  1946\t    if (maskActive && mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) continue;\n  1947\t    if (wet[i] < preset.wetLevel) wet[i] = preset.wetLevel;\n  1948\t    for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {\n  1949\t      const dval = d[k][i];\n  1950\t      if (dval < 0.001) continue;\n  1951\t      const lift = dval * preset.lift;\n  1952\t      d[k][i] = dval - lift;\n  1953\t      const nv = g[k][i] + lift;\n  1954\t      g[k][i] = nv > MAX_PIGMENT ? MAX_PIGMENT : nv;\n  1955\t    }\n  1956\t  }\n  1957\t\n  1958\t  // -- 2. Pick epicenters. If caller passed coords, use those; otherwise\n  1959\t  //       rejection-sample from current pigment density.\n  1960\t  let epicenters;\n  1961\t  if (coords) {\n  1962\t    // Use exactly the points the caller specified, preserving any\n  1963\t    // per-point radius/velocity/pressure/lift overrides.\n  1964\t    epicenters = coords.map((pt) => ({\n  1965\t      x:        pt.x,\n  1966\t      y:        pt.y,\n  1967\t      radius:   pt.radius   ?? null,\n  1968\t      velocity: pt.velocity ?? null,\n  1969\t      pressure: pt.pressure ?? null,\n  1970\t      lift:     pt.lift     ?? null,\n  1971\t    }));\n  1972\t  } else {\n  1973\t    // Auto-pick. Same rejection-sampling logic as v0.24.\n  1974\t    const g0 = g[0], g1 = g[1], g2 = g[2];\n  1975\t    let maxPigment = 0;\n  1976\t    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\n  1977\t      const p = g0[i] + g1[i] + g2[i];\n  1978\t      if (p > maxPigment) maxPigment = p;\n  1979\t    }\n  1980\t    const hasPigment = maxPigment > 0.05;\n  1981\t\n  1982\t    const range = preset.epiMax - preset.epiMin;\n  1983\t    const numEpicenters = preset.epiMin + Math.floor(Math.random() * (range + 1));\n  1984\t    epicenters = [];\n  1985\t    for (let ec = 0; ec < numEpicenters; ec++) {\n  1986\t      let cx, cy;\n  1987\t      if (hasPigment) {\n  1988\t        let found = false;\n  1989\t        for (let tries = 0; tries < 200; tries++) {\n  1990\t          const i = Math.floor(Math.random() * N);\n  1991\t          const p = (g0[i] + g1[i] + g2[i]) / maxPigment;\n  1992\t          if (Math.random() < p) {\n  1993\t            cx = i % GW;\n  1994\t            cy = Math.floor(i / GW);\n  1995\t            found = true;\n  1996\t            break;\n  1997\t          }\n  1998\t        }\n  1999\t        if (!found) {\n  2000\t          cx = Math.floor(Math.random() * GW);\n  2001\t          cy = Math.floor(Math.random() * GH);\n  2002\t        }\n  2003\t      } else {\n  2004\t        cx = Math.floor(Math.random() * GW);\n  2005\t        cy = Math.floor(Math.random() * GH);\n  2006\t      }\n  2007\t      epicenters.push({ x: cx, y: cy, radius: null, velocity: null,\n  2008\t                        pressure: null, lift: null });\n  2009\t    }\n  2010\t  }\n  2011\t\n  2012\t  // -- 3. Inject outward-radial velocity + pressure from each epicenter.\n  2013\t  //       Each epicenter resolves its own radius/velocity/pressure: per-\n  2014\t  //       point overrides win, otherwise fall back to the preset.\n  2015\t  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  2016\t  const pxToGrid = GW / rect.width;\n  2017\t\n  2018\t  for (const ec of epicenters) {\n  2019\t    const ecx = ec.x, ecy = ec.y;\n  2020\t    // Per-point overrides; if not set, use the preset's values.\n  2021\t    // v2 \u2014 scale radiusPx by canvas-size factor so splash zones stay\n  2022\t    // visually proportional on small canvases (matches paintAt scaling).\n  2023\t    const radiusPx = (ec.radius   != null ? ec.radius   : preset.radiusPx) * _canvasScale;\n  2024\t    const velMag   = ec.velocity != null ? ec.velocity : preset.velocity;\n  2025\t    const presMag  = ec.pressure != null ? ec.pressure : preset.pressure;\n  2026\t    const radiusGrid = radiusPx * pxToGrid;\n  2027\t    const r2 = radiusGrid * radiusGrid;\n  2028\t    const minX = Math.max(0, Math.floor(ecx - radiusGrid));\n  2029\t    const maxX = Math.min(GW - 1, Math.ceil(ecx + radiusGrid));\n  2030\t    const minY = Math.max(0, Math.floor(ecy - radiusGrid));\n[6743 lines total]",

MAX_PIGMENT : nv;\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // -- 2. Pick epicenters. Same rejection-sampling logic as v0.24.\\n    const g0 = g[0], g1 = g[1], g2 = g[2];\\n    let maxPigment = 0;\\n    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\\n      const p = g0[i] + g1[i] + g2[i];\\n      if (p > maxPigment) maxPigment = p;\\n    }\\n    const hasPigment = maxPigment > 0.05;\\n\\n    const range = preset.epiMax - preset.epiMin;\\n    const numEpicenters = preset.epiMin + Math.floor(Math.random() * (range + 1));\\n    epicenters = [];\\n    for (let ec = 0; ec < numEpicenters; ec++) {\\n      let cx, cy;\\n      if (hasPigment) {\\n        let found = false;\\n        for (let tries = 0; tries < 200; tries++) {\\n          const i = Math.floor(Math.random() * N);\\n          const p = (g0[i] + g1[i] + g2[i]) / maxPigment;\\n          if (Math.random() < p) {\\n            cx = i % GW;\\n            cy = Math.floor(i / GW);\\n            found = true;\\n            break;\\n          }\\n        }\\n        if (!found) {\\n          cx = Math.floor(Math.random() * GW);\\n          cy = Math.floor(Math.random() * GH);\\n        }\\n      } else {\\n        cx = Math.floor(Math.random() * GW);\\n        cy = Math.floor(Math.random() * GH);\\n      }\\n      epicenters.push({ x: cx, y: cy, radius: null, velocity: null,\\n                        pressure: null, lift: null });\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // -- 3. Inject outward-radial velocity + pressure from each epicenter.\\n  //       Each epicenter resolves its own radius/velocity/pressure: per-\\n  //       point overrides win, otherwise fall back to the preset.\\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\\n  const pxToGrid = GW / rect.width;\\n\\n  for (const ec of epicenters) {\\n    const ecx = ec.x, ecy = ec.y;\\n    // Per-point overrides; if not set, use the preset's values.\\n    // v2 \\u2014 scale radiusPx by canvas-size factor so splash zones stay\\n    // visually proportional on small canvases (matches paintAt scaling).\\n    const radiusPx = (ec.radius   != null ? ec.radius   : preset.radiusPx) * _canvasScale;\\n    const velMag   = ec.velocity != null ? ec.velocity : preset.velocity;\\n    const presMag  = ec.pressure != null ? ec.pressure : preset.pressure;\\n    const radiusGrid = radiusPx * pxToGrid;\\n    const r2 = radiusGrid * radiusGrid;\\n    const minX = Math.max(0, Math.floor(ecx - radiusGrid));\\n    const maxX = Math.min(GW - 1, Math.ceil(ecx + radiusGrid));\\n    const minY = Math.max(0, Math.floor(ecy - radiusGrid));\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor-lib.js\"}"

      "new_str": "  // State snapshot\n  state() {\n    return {\n      gridWidth: GW, gridHeight: GH,\n      displayWidth: DISPLAY_W, displayHeight: DISPLAY_H,\n      brushSize: brushSize,\n      pigment: currentPigment,\n      canvasScale: _canvasScale,\n      animationMode: typeof animationMode !== 'undefined' ? animationMode : 'off',\n      visualizationMode: typeof visualizationMode !== 'undefined' ? visualizationMode : 'off',\n      backgroundRunning: typeof timeWash !== 'undefined' && timeWash !== null,\n      edgeDarkening: edgeDarkeningEnabled,\n      fadePainting: fadeEnabled,\n      pauseDrying: typeof dryingPaused !== 'undefined' ? dryingPaused : false,\n      gouacheMode: _gouacheMode,\n    };\n  },\n\n  // v0.52 \u2014 Preset save/load. Captures every user-controllable setting\n  // (brush, paper, evaporation, modes, fade, animation, etc.) into a\n  // plain serializable object that can be JSON-stringified, saved to\n  // localStorage, shared between sessions, posted to a gist, etc.\n  //\n  // Excludes: the actual painted artwork (d[]/g[] arrays \u2014 that's\n  // *content*, not settings), cursor state, system-dependent toggles\n  // (WebGL, mobile mode), and debug instrumentation (perf overlay).\n  //\n  // The shape is documented by `version: 1`. Future preset formats\n  // can bump the version and applyPreset can migrate forward; v1\n  // presets will continue to apply correctly to forward-compatible\n  // lib versions (unknown fields are silently ignored).\n  getPreset() {\n    // Derive evaporation multiplier from the stored rate.\n    // setEvaporationMult does evapRate = 0.9988^mult, so:\n    //   mult = log(rate) / log(0.9988)\n    // Round to avoid tiny float artifacts in serialization.\n    let evapMult = Math.log(evaporationRate) / Math.log(0.9988);\n    if (!isFinite(evapMult)) evapMult = 1;\n    evapMult = Math.round(evapMult * 100) / 100;\n    return {\n      version: 1,\n      // Brush\n      brushSize:     brushSize,\n      pigment:       currentPigment,\n      pressure:      _brushPressure,\n      flow:          _brushFlow,\n      paintLoad:     paintLoadMult,\n      waterLoad:     waterLoadMult,\n      // Paper / sim\n      paperColor:    { r: PAPER_R_BASE, g: PAPER_G_BASE, b: PAPER_B_BASE },\n      evaporation:   evapMult,\n      gouacheMode:   _gouacheMode,\n      edgeDarkening: edgeDarkeningEnabled,\n      pauseDrying:   dryingPaused,\n      transparent:   transparentBg,\n      continuousFlow: _continuousFlow,\n      // Fade\n      fadePainting:   fadeEnabled,\n      fadeHalfLifeMs: fadeHalfLifeMs,\n      // Animation / visualization\n      animationMode:     typeof animationMode !== 'undefined' ? animationMode : 'off',\n      visualizationMode: typeof visualizationMode !== 'undefined' ? visualizationMode : 'off',\n    };\n  },\n\n  // applyPreset(obj) \u2014 restore settings from a preset object. Each\n  // field is applied via the existing public API method, so all the\n  // side effects (events, re-LERPs, re-renders, swatch refreshes)\n  // fire naturally. Missing fields are skipped; unknown fields are\n  // ignored. Order matters in a few cases:\n  //   - paperColor before gouacheMode, so auto-LERP uses the new paper\n  //   - animation/visualization last, since they may inject state\n  //     that depends on other settings (paper color, pigment choice)\n  // After all fields are applied, dispatches 'presetapplied' on the\n  // host element so wiring can update sliders, swatches, toggles,\n  // etc. to match the new state.\n  applyPreset(p) {\n    if (!p || typeof p !== 'object') {\n      throw new Error('applyPreset: expected a preset object');\n    }\n    if (p.version !== undefined && p.version > 1) {\n      console.warn('applyPreset: preset version', p.version,\n        'is newer than this lib understands (v1). Applying best-effort.');\n    }\n    // Paper first \u2014 affects gouacheMode('auto') LERP downstream.\n    if (p.paperColor && typeof p.paperColor === 'object') {\n      api.paperColor(p.paperColor.r, p.paperColor.g, p.paperColor.b);\n    }\n    if (p.evaporation !== undefined)    api.evaporation(p.evaporation);\n    if (p.gouacheMode !== undefined)    api.gouacheMode(p.gouacheMode);\n    // Brush\n    if (p.brushSize !== undefined)      api.brushSize(p.brushSize);\n    if (p.pigment !== undefined)        api.pigment(p.pigment);\n    if (p.pressure !== undefined)       api.pressure(p.pressure);\n    if (p.flow !== undefined)           api.flow(p.flow);\n    if (p.paintLoad !== undefined)      api.paintLoad(p.paintLoad);\n    if (p.waterLoad !== undefined)      api.waterLoad(p.waterLoad);\n    // Sim toggles\n    if (p.edgeDarkening !== undefined)  api.edgeDarkening(p.edgeDarkening);\n    if (p.pauseDrying !== undefined)    api.pauseDrying(p.pauseDrying);\n    if (p.transparent !== undefined)    api.transparent(p.transparent);\n    if (p.continuousFlow !== undefined) api.continuousFlow(p.continuousFlow);\n    // Fade\n    if (p.fadeHalfLifeMs !== undefined) api.fadeHalfLife(p.fadeHalfLifeMs);\n    if (p.fadePainting !== undefined)   api.fadePainting(p.fadePainting);\n    // Animation / visualization last\n    if (p.animationMode !== undefined)\n      api.setAnimation(p.animationMode === 'off' ? null : p.animationMode);\n    if (p.visualizationMode !== undefined)\n      api.setVisualization(p.visualizationMode === 'off' ? null : p.visualizationMode);\n    targetEl.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('presetapplied', { detail: { preset: p } }));\n    return api;\n  },",
      "old_str": "  // State snapshot\n  state() {\n    return {\n      gridWidth: GW, gridHeight: GH,\n      displayWidth: DISPLAY_W, displayHeight: DISPLAY_H,\n      brushSize: brushSize,\n      pigment: currentPigment,\n      canvasScale: _canvasScale,\n      animationMode: typeof animationMode !== 'undefined' ? animationMode : 'off',\n      visualizationMode: typeof visualizationMode !== 'undefined' ? visualizationMode : 'off',\n      backgroundRunning: typeof timeWash !== 'undefined' && timeWash !== null,\n      edgeDarkening: edgeDarkeningEnabled,\n      fadePainting: fadeEnabled,\n      pauseDrying: typeof dryingPaused !== 'undefined' ? dryingPaused : false,\n      gouacheMode: _gouacheMode,\n    };\n  },",

OFF (default) \u2014 transparent watercolor pigments (Quinacridone Rose, Hansa Yellow, Cerulean Blue). Looks great on white/cream paper, nearly invisible on black. ON \u2014 opaque gouache pigments with high light-scattering (S) values, vibrant on any paper. AUTO (v0.51+) \u2014 LERPs between the two sets based on the perceptual luminance of the current paper color, so dragging the paper picker from cream to charcoal smoothly morphs the pigments from transparent watercolor to fully opaque gouache. The physically natural choice: light paper barely needs scattering pigments (transparent works); dark paper requires scattering (opaque works); papers in between get a proportional mix. Imagine a cup of water spilled violently across the page, or a pressure washer hitting the surface: a single point of impact with enough water, velocity, and pressure to push pigment anywhere on the canvas. Three modes selected by modifier key. Click alone \u2192 'water' (a single splash that lifts deposited pigment back into suspension and diffuses it outward, like a rinse). Shift-click \u2192 'pigment' (opaque wash in the currently-selected pigment color, like spilling colored paint). Alt-click \u2192 'paper' (clears the area back to canvas color, like an eraser). Where Big Splash distributes chaos across 4\\u20138 epicenters, Deluge concentrates it into ONE giant event \\u2014 flooded paper, strong radial flow at high velocity (~2.3\\u00d7 Big Splash), and a hard pressure spike that drives flow well beyond the splash ring. Imagine a cup of water spilled violently across the page, or a pressure washer hitting the surface: a single point of impact with enough water, velocity, and pressure to push pigment anywhere on the canvas. Lifts 95% of deposited pigment.\\\">Deluge<span class=\\\"info-marker\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\u24d8</span></button>\\n      <button class=\\\"btn\\\"       id=\\\"obliterate-btn\\\"\\n              data-tip=\\\"Obliterate: destroys the current drawing via a wet wash centered on the canvas. Three modes selected by modifier key. Click alone \\u2192 'water' (a single splash that lifts deposited pigment back into suspension and diffuses it outward, like a rinse). Shift-click \\u2192 'pigment' (opaque wash in the currently-selected pigment color, like spilling colored paint). Alt-click \\u2192 'paper' (clears the area back to canvas color, like an eraser). All complete in 500ms. Equivalent to Watercolor.obliterate({ mode, durationMs: 500 }).\\\">Obliterate<span class=\\\"info-marker\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\u24d8</span></button>\\n      <button class=\\\"btn\\\"       id=\\\"dry-btn\\\"\\n              data-tip=\\\"Instantly evaporates all water. Any suspended pigment locks in place as deposited.\\\">Dry<span class=\\\"info-marker\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\u24d8</span></button>\\n      <button class=\\\"btn\\\"       id=\\\"remove-mask-btn\\\"\\n              data-tip=\\\"Removes all masking fluid in one go \\u2014 like rubbing it off a dry painting. Reveals whatever was frozen underneath.\\\">Remove mask<span class=\\\"info-marker\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\u24d8</span></button>\\n      <button class=\\\"btn\\\"       id=\\\"export-btn\\\"\\n              data-tip=\\\"Saves the current canvas as a PNG. Composites the paper color underneath unless Transparent is on.\\\">Export PNG<span class=\\\"info-marker\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\u24d8</span></button>\\n      <button class=\\\"btn\\\"       id=\\\"reset\\\"\\n              data-tip=\\\"Clears all pigment and returns the canvas to fresh wet paper.\\\">Reset<span class=\\\"info-marker\\\" aria-hidden=\\\"true\\\">\\u24d8</span></button>\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor.html\"}"

{};\n    const obj = JSON.parse(raw);\n    return obj && typeof obj === 'object' ? obj : {};\n  } catch (e) {\n    console.warn('preset load: corrupt localStorage, ignoring', e);\n    return {};\n  }\n}\nfunction saveUserPresets(map) {\n  try {\n    localStorage.setItem(PRESETS_LS_KEY, JSON.stringify(map));\n  } catch (e) {\n    console.warn('preset save: localStorage write failed', e);\n  }\n}\n\nconst presetsSelect = document.getElementById('presets-select');\nconst presetsSave   = document.getElementById('presets-save');\nconst presetsDelete = any exist)\n  const userNames = Object.keys(user);\n  if (userNames.length) {\n    const ogUser = document.createElement('optgroup');\n    ogUser.label = 'Saved';\n    for (const name of userNames) {\n      const opt = document.createElement('option');\n      opt.value = name;\n      opt.textContent = name;\n      ogUser.appendChild(opt);\n    }\n    presetsSelect.appendChild(ogUser);\n  }\n  if (selectName) {\n    presetsSelect.value = selectName;\n  }\n}\n\nfunction applyPresetByName(name) {\n  if (!name) return;\n  const user = loadUserPresets();\n  const preset = BUILTIN_PRESETS[name] || user[name];\n  if (!preset) return;\n  try {\n    wc.applyPreset(preset);\n  } catch (e) {\n    console.error('preset apply failed:', e);\n    alert('Failed to apply preset: ' + (e.message || e));\n  }\n}\n\nif (presetsSelect) {\n  populatePresetsDropdown();\n  presetsSelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n    applyPresetByName(presetsSelect.value);\n  });\n}\n\nif (presetsSave) {\n '');\n    if (name == null) return;\n    const trimmed = name.trim();\n    if (!trimmed) return;\n    if (BUILTIN_NAMES.has(trimmed)) {\n      alert('\"' + trimmed + '\" is a built-in preset name. Pick a different name.');\n      return;\n    }\n    const user = loadUserPresets();\n    user[trimmed] = wc.getPreset();\n    saveUserPresets(user);\n    populatePresetsDropdown(trimmed);\n  });\n}\n\nif (presetsDelete) {\n  presetsDelete.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    const name = presetsSelect && presetsSelect.value;\n    if (!name) {\n      alert('Select a preset first.');\n      return;\n    }\n    if (BUILTIN_NAMES.has(name)) {\n      alert('Built-in presets can\\'t be deleted.');\n      return;\n    }\n    const user = loadUserPresets();\n    if (!user[name]) {\n      alert('Preset not found.');\n      return;\n    }\n    if (!confirm('Delete preset \"' + name + '\"? This cannot be undone.')) return;\n    delete user[name];\n    saveUserPresets(user);\n    populatePresetsDropdown();\n  });\n}\n\n// v0.52 \u2014 listen for presetapplied events to refresh other UI bits\n// (sliders, swatches, toggles) so they reflect the loaded state.\nhost.addEventListener('presetapplied', () => {\n  // Re-sync everything that mirrors lib state into the DOM. Second: a fourth splash preset, \"deluge,\"\n          which concentrates the chaos of a Big Splash into one\n          overwhelming event &mdash; the visual of a cup of water\n          violently spilled across the page, or a pressure washer\n          hitting the surface.\n        </p>\n        <h3>Presets</h3>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>New <code>getPreset()</code> / <code>applyPreset(obj)</code> APIs.</strong> Capture every user-controllable setting (brush size, pigment, pressure, flow, paint/water load, paper color, evaporation, gouache mode, fade settings, animation/visualization modes, edge darkening, drying, transparent bg, continuous flow &mdash; 18 fields total) into a plain serializable object. JSON-clean (~350 bytes), round-trips bit-exact, cross-instance compatible. Excludes painted artwork (the d[]/g[] arrays are <em>content</em>, not <em>settings</em>) and system-dependent toggles (WebGL, mobile, perf overlay).</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>New \"Presets\" control at the top of the panel.</strong> A <code>&lt;select&gt;</code> dropdown with two optgroups: built-in starter presets (Default, Dark mode, Wet &amp; loose, Dry brush) and a \"Saved\" group of user-saved presets, persisted in <code>localStorage['watercolor.presets.v1']</code>. Picking a preset applies it immediately; \"Save\u2026\" prompts for a name (overwrites if it matches an existing user preset, rejects if it collides with a built-in); \"Delete\" removes the selected user preset with a confirmation. Built-ins are protected from deletion.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Applied presets fire <code>'presetapplied'</code> on the host element.</strong> The wiring listens and re-syncs all the affected DOM controls in one pass &mdash; brush slider, paint/water load sliders, fade halflife slider, paper color picker, the gouache tri-state button, and every toggle. So selecting \"Dark mode (gouache, auto)\" from the dropdown not only changes the lib state but also visibly flips every control in the panel to match.</li>\n          <li><strong>Apply order matters and is enforced.</strong> Paper color is applied <em>before</em> gouache mode so that <code>gouacheMode('auto')</code> recomputes its LERP from the new paper darkness. Animation and visualization are applied last because they may inject state that depends on other settings. Missing fields are skipped (forward-compat with old presets); unknown fields are ignored (forward-compat with future-version presets).</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-fix\">fix</span> <strong>Added the long-claimed <code>evaporation(mult)</code> method.</strong> The docs have referenced <code>Watercolor.evaporation(value)</code> since v0.30 (accepts a preset name string OR a numeric multiplier), but the actual API only had <code>paperWetness(presetName)</code> &mdash; the numeric path was never wired up. Now it is. <code>evaporation('damp')</code> forwards to <code>paperWetness</code>; <code>evaporation(8)</code> sets the multiplier directly; <code>evaporation()</code> returns the current effective multiplier (inverted from the stored rate). Required by <code>applyPreset</code> to round-trip evaporation cleanly.</li>\n        </ul>\n        <h3>Deluge splash</h3>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>New \"deluge\" splash preset.</strong> Fourth entry in <code>SPLASH_PRESETS</code>, joining default / bigSplash / fineSpritz. Single epicenter (<code>epiMin: 1, epiMax: 1</code>), 900px radius covering most of the canvas, velocity 2.5 (~2.3&times; bigSplash), pressure 1.2 (~2.2&times; bigSplash), lift 0.95, wetLevel 1.0. The result: a flooded canvas with strong outward-radial flow that pushes pigment well beyond the visible splash ring. Mass conservation verified: 95% of deposited pigment ends up suspended after one deluge, total mass unchanged.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>UI Deluge button</strong> next to Big Splash and Fine Spritz. Centers the epicenter at canvas center for a predictable \"cup spilled in the middle\" effect, rather than the rejection-sampler's pigment-biased random pick. Programmatic callers can still pass explicit coords: <code>wc.splash([{x: 500, y: 300}], 'deluge')</code>.</li>\n          <li><strong>When to use which splash:</strong> <em>fineSpritz</em> for soft texture-adding bursts; <em>default</em> or <em>bigSplash</em> for distributed chaos that mixes everything; <em>deluge</em> for a single dramatic event when you want a directional rush of water across a finished wash.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.51 ===== -->",

The overlay\n          shows p50/p95/p99 frame times, per-phase ms breakdown,\n          active cell count, heap delta (Chromium-only), and Long\n          Animation Frames.\n        </p>\n\n        <pre class=\"docs-code\"><span class=\"docs-sig\">Watercolor.webglAvailable()</span> \u2192 bool</pre>\n        <p>\n          Tests for WebGL2 support. Lazy-inits on first call. Use this\n          before calling <code>Watercolor.webgl(true)</code> to know\n          whether the toggle will succeed.\n        </p>\n\n        <h2 id=\"api-presets\">Presets (v0.52+)</h2>\n        <pre class=\"docs-code\"><span class=\"docs-sig\">Watercolor.getPreset()</span> \u2192 preset object\n<span class=\"docs-sig\">Watercolor.applyPreset(obj)</span> \u2192 instance</pre>\n        <p>\n          Capture and restore every user-controllable setting. The\n          returned preset object is a plain JSON-serializable snapshot\n          (~350 bytes) covering 18 fields:\n        </p>\n        <pre class=\"docs-code\">{\n  version: 1,\n  // Brush\n  brushSize, pigment, pressure, flow, paintLoad, waterLoad,\n  // Paper / sim\n  paperColor: { r, g, b },\n  evaporation,        // numeric multiplier\n  gouacheMode,        // false | true | 'auto'\n  edgeDarkening, pauseDrying, transparent, continuousFlow,\n  // Fade\n  fadePainting, fadeHalfLifeMs,\n  // Animation / visualization\n  animationMode, visualizationMode\n}</pre>\n        <p>\n          Excludes painted artwork (the d[]/g[] arrays are\n          <em>content</em>, not <em>settings</em>) and system-dependent\n          toggles (WebGL, mobile mode, perf overlay). Apply order is\n          enforced: paper color first (so <code>gouacheMode('auto')</code>\n          recomputes its LERP from the new paper darkness), then\n          brush, then toggles, then fade, then animation/visualization\n          last. Missing fields are skipped (forward-compat with old\n          presets); unknown fields are ignored (forward-compat with\n          future-version presets).\n        </p>\n        <p>\n          Applying a preset fires <code>'presetapplied'</code> on the\n          host element with <code>detail: { preset }</code>. Useful\n          for hosts that mirror lib state into DOM controls (sliders,\n          swatches, toggles) \u2014 listen and re-sync.\n        </p>\n        <pre class=\"docs-code\">// Save / load via localStorage\nlocalStorage.setItem('myPreset', JSON.stringify(wc.getPreset()));\n\nconst saved = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('myPreset'));\nwc.applyPreset(saved);\n\n// Apply across instances\nconst wc2 = Watercolor.create(otherEl);\nwc2.applyPreset(wc.getPreset());\n\n// React to applied presets\nhostEl.addEventListener('presetapplied', (e) => {\n  console.log('applied preset:', e.detail.preset);\n});</pre>\n\n        <h2 id=\"api-coords\">Coordinates</h2>",

    "type": "text",
    "text": "**v0.52 shipped.** Both features wired, documented, and verified.\n\n## Presets\n\nA new \"Presets\" control at the top of the panel \u2014 `<select>` dropdown with two optgroups:\n\n**Built-in (always available, can't be deleted):**\n- *Default (factory)* \u2014 back to the original settings\n- *Dark mode (gouache, auto)* \u2014 black paper, auto-gouache, blue pigment, larger brush\n- *Wet & loose (flooded)* \u2014 big brush, flooded evaporation, lower strength\n- *Dry brush (boneDry)* \u2014 small brush, high pressure, continuous flow off\n\n**Saved (your own, persisted to localStorage):**\n- Empty by default. \"Save\u2026\" prompts for a name; \"Delete\" confirms before removing.\n\nSelecting any name applies it immediately \u2014 the whole panel visibly resyncs (sliders move, swatches update, toggles flip, paper picker recolors, gouache tri-state switches). The painted artwork stays untouched.\n\n### What `getPreset()` captures\n\n```js\n{\n  version: 1,\n  // Brush\n  brushSize, pigment, pressure, flow, paintLoad, waterLoad,\n  // Paper / sim\n  paperColor: { r, g, b },\n  evaporation,             // numeric multiplier\n  gouacheMode,             // false | true | 'auto'\n  edgeDarkening, pauseDrying, transparent, continuousFlow,\n  // Fade\n  fadePainting, fadeHalfLifeMs,\n  // Animation / visualization\n  animationMode, visualizationMode\n}\n```\n\n18 fields, ~350 bytes serialized. Round-trip verified bit-exact through JSON; cross-instance apply verified.\n\n### Apply order matters\n\nI enforce a specific order in `applyPreset`:\n1. **Paper color first** \u2014 so that downstream `gouacheMode('auto')` recomputes its LERP using the new paper darkness, not the old one\n2. Evaporation\n3. Gouache mode (now sees the right paper)\n4. Brush settings\n5. Sim toggles\n6. Fade\n7. **Animation/visualization last** \u2014 they may inject state that depends on paper, pigment, etc.\n\nMissing fields are skipped (forward-compat with old presets); unknown fields are ignored (forward-compat with future versions).\n\n### The bug I fixed along the way\n\n`Watercolor.evaporation(value)` has been in the docs since v0.30 \u2014 but it was never actually wired up. The lib only had `paperWetness('damp')` etc., with no numeric path. Pre-v0.52 code passing `evaporation(8)` silently no-op'd. Required for preset round-trip to work cleanly, so I added it: accepts preset names AND numerics now, returns the current multiplier when called with no arg.\n\n## Deluge splash\n\nFourth entry in `SPLASH_PRESETS`, alongside default / bigSplash / fineSpritz:\n\n```js\ndeluge: {\n  lift:        0.95,   // lift almost everything\n  wetLevel:    1.0,    // flooded\n  velocity:    2.5,    // ~2.3\u00d7 bigSplash\n  pressure:    1.2,    // ~2.2\u00d7 bigSplash\n  radiusPx:    900,    // covers most of the canvas\n  epiMin:      1,\n  epiMax:      1,      // single overwhelming event\n}\n```\n\nVerified end-to-end with synthetic paint + splash: mass conservation holds (1340.43 units before and after), exactly 95% of the deposited pigment ends up suspended in one event.\n\nThe UI button centers the epicenter at canvas center for the predictable \"cup spilled in the middle\" feel, instead of letting the rejection sampler pick a pigment-biased random spot. Programmatic callers can still pass explicit coords:\n\n```js\nwc.splash([{x: 500, y: 300}], 'deluge');\n```\n\n### When to use which splash\n\n| Effect | When |\n|---|---|\n| **fineSpritz** | Soft texture-adding bursts \u2014 flicked water from a brush |\n| **default** | Distributed chaos, ~5 epicenters mixing |\n| **bigSplash** | Bucket of water dropped on the canvas |\n| **deluge** | A single dramatic event \u2014 cup spilled, pressure washer hitting the page |\n\n## API additions to the changelog\n\n```js\n// New in v0.52\nwc.evaporation(8);              // numeric multiplier\nwc.evaporation('damp');         // or a preset name\nwc.splash('deluge');            // single-epicenter overwhelming\nwc.getPreset();                 // settings snapshot\nwc.applyPreset(obj);            // restore\n// + 'presetapplied' event fires on the host element\n```\n\n## Note on what's intentional vs. accidental\n\nThe `presetapplied` listener in the wiring resyncs every relevant control in one pass \u2014 sliders, swatches, paper picker, all the toggles, the gouache tri-state, fade halflife. That's a substantial chunk of code but it's the right place for it: any preset can change any setting, and the UI needs to mirror lib state. Without the listener, picking \"Dark mode\" from the dropdown would change the painting but leave every slider where it was, which would be the kind of subtle UX bug that's annoying to diagnose later.",
    "citations": []
  }
]

OFF (default) \u2014 transparent watercolor pigments (Quinacridone Rose, Hansa Yellow, Cerulean Blue). Looks great on white/cream paper, nearly invisible on black. ON \u2014 opaque gouache pigments with high light-scattering (S) values, vibrant on any paper. AUTO (v0.51+) \u2014 LERPs between the two sets based on the perceptual luminance of the current paper color, so dragging the paper picker from cream to charcoal smoothly morphs the pigments from transparent watercolor to fully opaque gouache. The physically natural choice: light paper barely needs scattering pigments (transparent works); dark paper requires scattering (opaque works); papers in between get a proportional mix. Affects both new strokes AND the rendering of already-deposited pigment.\">Gouache: Off<span class=\"info-marker\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\u24d8</span></button>\n  1495\t      <button class=\"btn\"       id=\"rewet-btn\"\n  1496\t              data-tip=\"Restores wetness across the whole canvas and lifts 30% of deposited pigment back into suspension so dried strokes become mobile again.\">Re-wet<span class=\"info-marker\" aria-hidden=\"true\">\u24d8</span></button>\n  1497\t      <button class=\"btn\"       id=\"big-splash-btn\"\n  1498\t              data-tip=\"Big Splash: dramatic dispersal across the canvas. Imagine a cup of water spilled violently across the page, or a pressure washer hitting the surface: a single point of impact with enough water, velocity, and pressure to push pigment anywhere on the canvas. Shift-click \u2192 'pigment' (opaque wash in the currently-selected pigment color, like spilling colored paint). Alt-click \u2192 'paper' (clears the area back to canvas color, like an eraser). Range 0..8000 \u2014 set to 0 (instant) to paint everything synchronously the moment the SVG loads.\">\n          <span class=\"brush-label\">In duration</span>\n          <input type=\"range\" id=\"svg-duration\" min=\"0\" max=\"8000\" step=\"100\" value=\"2000\" />\n          <span class=\"brush-value\" id=\"svg-duration-value\">2000 ms</span>\n        </div>\n        <div class=\"brush-control\" data-tip=\"Easing curve for the drawing animation. Linear paints at a uniform rate (predictable but mechanical). EaseOut starts fast and slows \u2014 natural for confident strokes. EaseInOut spends time at both ends \u2014 feels deliberate. PenStroke combines short ramp-up + steady middle + short ramp-down, mimicking how a hand draws. EaseIn starts slow and accelerates \u2014 rarely useful for drawing but here for completeness.\">\n          <span class=\"brush-label\">In easing</span>\n          <select id=\"svg-easing\" class=\"select-control\">\n            <option value=\"linear\">linear</option>\n            <option value=\"easeIn\">easeIn</option>\n            <option value=\"easeOut\">easeOut</option>\n            <option value=\"easeInOut\">easeInOut</option>\n            <option value=\"penStroke\" selected>penStroke</option>\n          </select>\n        </div>\n        <div class=\"brush-control\" data-tip=\"Pause in milliseconds between SVG path elements. PenStroke combines short ramp-up + steady middle + short ramp-down, mimicking how a hand draws. Stored in\n// DISPLAY pixels (what the user types into the inputs); converted to\n// grid coords on demand for the lib calls. Centered on the canvas by\n// default and re-centered when the canvas resizes.\nconst containerWInput = document.getElementById('container-w');\nconst containerHInput = document.getElementById('container-h');\nconst containerOverlay = document.getElementById('container-overlay');\nconst containerShowBtn = document.getElementById('toggle-container-show');\n\nlet _containerVisible = true;\nlet _containerW = 400;\nlet _containerH = 400;\n\nfunction positionContainerOverlay() {\n  if (!containerOverlay) return;\n  const rect = host.getBoundingClientRect();\n  // Container is centered on the canvas at the current size.\n  const cx = rect.width  * 0.5;\n  const cy = rect.height * 0.5;\n  containerOverlay.style.left   = (cx - _containerW * 0.5) + 'px';\n  containerOverlay.style.top    = (cy - _containerH * 0.5) + 'px';\n  containerOverlay.style.width  = _containerW + 'px';\n  containerOverlay.style.height = _containerH + 'px';\n  containerOverlay.hidden = !_containerVisible;\n}\n\n// Convert the current container rect from DISPLAY pixels to GRID coords.\n// The lib's traceSVG bounds option expects grid units. Returns null if\n// the container is hidden (so callers can fall back to the default fit).\nfunction currentContainerGridRect() {\n  if (!_containerVisible) return null;\n  const rect = host.getBoundingClientRect();\n  if (rect.width === 0 || rect.height === 0) return null;\n  const s = wc.state();\n  const pxToGrid = s.gridWidth / rect.width;\n  const cxPx = rect.width  * 0.5;\n  const cyPx = rect.height * 0.5;\n  const xPx = cxPx - _containerW * 0.5;\n  const yPx = cyPx - _containerH * 0.5;\n  return {\n    x: xPx * pxToGrid,\n    y: yPx * pxToGrid,\n    w: _containerW * pxToGrid,\n    h: _containerH * pxToGrid,\n  };\n}\n\nif (containerWInput) {\n  containerWInput.addEventListener('input', () => {\n    const v = Math.max(50, Math.min(2000, +containerWInput.value || 400));\n    _containerW = v;\n    positionContainerOverlay();\n  });\n}\nif (containerHInput) {\n  containerHInput.addEventListener('input', () => {\n    const v = Math.max(50, Math.min(2000, +containerHInput.value || 400));\n    _containerH = v;\n    positionContainerOverlay();\n  });\n}\nif (containerShowBtn) {\n  containerShowBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    _containerVisible = !_containerVisible;\n    containerShowBtn.classList.toggle('on', _containerVisible);\n    containerShowBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(_containerVisible));\n    containerShowBtn.textContent = _containerVisible ? 'Show' : 'Hidden';\n    if (_containerVisible) {\n      containerShowBtn.classList.add('on');\n      containerShowBtn.textContent = 'Show';\n    } else {\n      containerShowBtn.classList.remove('on');\n      containerShowBtn.textContent = 'Hidden';\n    }\n    positionContainerOverlay();\n  });\n}\n// Reposition on window resize so the overlay stays centered.\nwindow.addEventListener('resize', positionContainerOverlay);\n// Initial position after layout settles.\nrequestAnimationFrame(positionContainerOverlay);\n\n// ============================================================\n// v0.53 \u2014 Deluge: tunable params + click-to-deluge mode\n// ============================================================\n// The deluge \"out transition\" is the primary thing the user iterates\n// on while tuning a SVG's exit feel. Four sliders override the built-\n// in 'deluge' preset's defaults per-trigger: velocity, pressure,\n// radius, lift. Click-to-deluge mode reroutes the next canvas click\n// from \"paint\" to \"deluge at click position, clamped to container.\"\nconst delugeVel    = document.getElementById('deluge-velocity');\nconst delugePress  = document.getElementById('deluge-pressure');\nconst delugeRadius = document.getElementById('deluge-radius');\nconst delugeLift   = document.getElementById('deluge-lift');\nconst delugeVelV   = document.getElementById('deluge-velocity-value');\nconst delugePressV = document.getElementById('deluge-pressure-value');\nconst delugeRadV   = document.getElementById('deluge-radius-value');\nconst delugeLiftV  = document.getElementById('deluge-lift-value');\n\nfunction bindDelugeSlider(slider, valueEl, decimals) {\n  if (!slider || !valueEl) return;\n  slider.addEventListener('input', () => {\n    valueEl.textContent = (+slider.value).toFixed(decimals);\n  });\n}\nbindDelugeSlider(delugeVel,    delugeVelV,   1);\nbindDelugeSlider(delugePress,  delugePressV, 1);\nbindDelugeSlider(delugeLift,   delugeLiftV,  2);\nif (delugeRadius && delugeRadV) {\n  delugeRadius.addEventListener('input', () => {\n    delugeRadV.textContent = String(delugeRadius.value);\n  });\n}\n\n// Compute the splash coord array for a deluge trigger. centerPx is\n// optional; when omitted, uses the container center. The returned\n// coords carry the slider overrides as per-point fields, so the\n// 'deluge' preset's defaults are overridden.\nfunction delugeCoords(centerPx) {\n  const rect = host.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const s = wc.state();\n  const pxToGrid = s.gridWidth / rect.width;\n  // Default center: container center (or canvas center if container hidden).\n  let cxPx, cyPx;\n  if (centerPx) {\n    cxPx = centerPx.x;\n    cyPx = centerPx.y;\n  } else {\n    cxPx = rect.width * 0.5;\n    cyPx = rect.height * 0.5;\n  }\n  // Clamp to container edges if container is visible.\n  if (_containerVisible) {\n    const minX = rect.width * 0.5 - _containerW * 0.5;\n    const maxX = rect.width * 0.5 + _containerW * 0.5;\n    const minY = rect.height * 0.5 - _containerH * 0.5;\n    const maxY = rect.height * 0.5 + _containerH * 0.5;\n    cxPx = Math.max(minX, Math.min(maxX, cxPx));\n    cyPx = Math.max(minY, Math.min(maxY, cyPx));\n  }\n  return [{\n    x: cxPx * pxToGrid,\n    y: cyPx * pxToGrid,\n    velocity: delugeVel    ? +delugeVel.value    : undefined,\n    pressure: delugePress  ? +delugePress.value  : undefined,\n    radius:   delugeRadius ? +delugeRadius.value : undefined,\n    lift:     delugeLift   ? +delugeLift.value   : undefined,\n  }];\n}\n\n// Manual Deluge button \u2014 centered on container (or canvas if hidden).\nconst delugeBtn = document.getElementById('deluge-btn');\nif (delugeBtn) {\n  delugeBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    wc.splash(delugeCoords(), 'deluge');\n    flash(delugeBtn);\n  });\n}\n\n// Click-to-deluge mode. Captures the NEXT click on the canvas-host\n// element, routes it to a deluge with click-position epicenter\n// (clamped to container), then turns itself back off so the next click\n// returns to paint behavior. A click-and-drag won't trigger; only a\n// clean click. Cursor changes to crosshair while armed (see CSS).\nconst delugeClickBtn = document.getElementById('toggle-deluge-click');\nlet _delugeArmed = false;\nfunction setDelugeMode(on) {\n  _delugeArmed = !!on;\n  if (delugeClickBtn) {\n    delugeClickBtn.classList.toggle('on', _delugeArmed);\n    delugeClickBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(_delugeArmed));\n  }\n  document.body.classList.toggle('ws-deluge-mode', _delugeArmed);\n}\nif (delugeClickBtn) {\n  delugeClickBtn.addEventListener('click', () => setDelugeMode(!_delugeArmed));\n}\n// Capture-phase listener on the canvas \u2014 fires BEFORE the lib's\n// pointer-paint handler so we can preventDefault/stopPropagation when\n// armed. Otherwise the click would also lay a paint dab.\nhost.addEventListener('pointerdown', (e) => {\n  if (!_delugeArmed) return;\n  // Only respond to the primary button.\n  if (e.button !== 0) return;\n  e.preventDefault();\n  e.stopPropagation();\n  const rect = host.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const cx = e.clientX - rect.left;\n  const cy = e.clientY - rect.top;\n  wc.splash(delugeCoords({ x: cx, y: cy }), 'deluge');\n  setDelugeMode(false);  // disarm after one shot\n}, { capture: true });\n\n// ============================================================\n// v0.53 \u2014 Brush preset save/load\n// ============================================================\n// Captures just the brush state \u2014 pigment + brushSize + paintLoad +\n// waterLoad + pressure + flow \u2014 into a separate localStorage slot.\n// Independent from the global Preset at the top so the user can swap\n// brushes mid-session without resetting paper, animation, SVG, etc.\nconst BRUSH_PRESETS_LS_KEY = 'watercolor.brush-presets.v1';\nconst BUILTIN_BRUSH_PRESETS = {\n  'Default': {\n    version: 1,\n    pigment: 0, brushSize: 28, paintLoad: 1, waterLoad: 1,\n    pressure: 0.7, flow: 0.4,\n  },\n  'Fine pen (SVG drawing)': {\n    version: 1,\n    pigment: 0, brushSize: 6, paintLoad: 1.4, waterLoad: 0.5,\n    pressure: 0.85, flow: 0.25,\n  },\n  'Wet wash': {\n    version: 1,\n    pigment: 0, brushSize: 70, paintLoad: 0.9, waterLoad: 1.6,\n    pressure: 0.5, flow: 0.4,\n  },\n  'Dry brush': {\n    version: 1,\n    pigment: 0, brushSize: 14, paintLoad: 1.3, waterLoad: 0.6,\n    pressure: 0.85, flow: 0.4,\n  },\n};\nconst BUILTIN_BRUSH_NAMES = new Set(Object.keys(BUILTIN_BRUSH_PRESETS));\n\nfunction loadUserBrushPresets() {\n  try {\n    const raw = localStorage.getItem(BRUSH_PRESETS_LS_KEY);\n    if (!raw) return {};\n    const obj = JSON.parse(raw);\n    return obj && typeof obj === 'object' ? obj : {};\n  } catch (e) {\n    return {};\n  }\n}\nfunction saveUserBrushPresets(map) {\n  try { localStorage.setItem(BRUSH_PRESETS_LS_KEY, JSON.stringify(map)); }\n  catch (e) { console.warn('brush preset save: localStorage write failed', e); }\n}\nfunction getCurrentBrushPreset() {\n  return {\n    version: 1,\n    pigment:   wc.pigment(),\n    brushSize: wc.brushSize(),\n    paintLoad: wc.paintLoad(),\n    waterLoad: wc.waterLoad(),\n    pressure:  wc.pressure(),\n    flow:      wc.flow(),\n  };\n}\nfunction applyBrushPreset(bp) {\n  if (!bp) return;\n  if (bp.pigment   !== undefined) wc.pigment(bp.pigment);\n  if (bp.brushSize !== undefined) wc.brushSize(bp.brushSize);\n  if (bp.paintLoad !== undefined) wc.paintLoad(bp.paintLoad);\n  if (bp.waterLoad !== undefined) wc.waterLoad(bp.waterLoad);\n  if (bp.pressure  !== undefined) wc.pressure(bp.pressure);\n  if (bp.flow      !== undefined) wc.flow(bp.flow);\n  // Re-sync the visible controls. We don't fire 'presetapplied' since\n  // that listener triggers a full UI rebuild; for a brush preset we\n  // only need to touch a few elements.\n  const sizeS = document.getElementById('size');\n  const sizeV = document.getElementById('size-value');\n  if (sizeS) sizeS.value = String(bp.brushSize);\n  if (sizeV) sizeV.textContent = String(bp.brushSize);\n  const plS = document.getElementById('paint-load');\n  const plV = document.getElementById('paint-load-value');\n  if (plS) plS.value = String(bp.paintLoad);\n  if (plV) plV.textContent = (+bp.paintLoad).toFixed(2);\n  const wlS = document.getElementById('water-load');\n  const wlV = document.getElementById('water-load-value');\n  if (wlS) wlS.value = String(bp.waterLoad);\n  if (wlV) wlV.textContent = (+bp.waterLoad).toFixed(2);\n  if (typeof rebuildSwatches === 'function') rebuildSwatches();\n  if (typeof updateCursorPreview === 'function') updateCursorPreview();\n}\n\nconst brushPresetSelect = document.getElementById('brush-preset-select');\nconst brushPresetSave   = document.getElementById('brush-preset-save');\nconst brushPresetDelete = document.getElementById('brush-preset-delete');\n\nfunction populateBrushPresetsDropdown(selectName) {\n  if (!brushPresetSelect) return;\n  const user = loadUserBrushPresets();\n  brushPresetSelect.innerHTML = '';\n  const ph = document.createElement('option');\n  ph.value = ''; ph.textContent = '\u2014 select \u2014';\n  brushPresetSelect.appendChild(ph);\n  const ogBuilt = document.createElement('optgroup');\n  ogBuilt.label = 'Built-in';\n  for (const name of Object.keys(BUILTIN_BRUSH_PRESETS)) {\n    const opt = document.createElement('option');\n    opt.value = name; opt.textContent = name;\n    ogBuilt.appendChild(opt);\n  }\n  brushPresetSelect.appendChild(ogBuilt);\n  const userNames = Object.keys(user);\n  if (userNames.length) {\n    const ogUser = document.createElement('optgroup');\n    ogUser.label = 'Saved';\n    for (const name of userNames) {\n      const opt = document.createElement('option');\n      opt.value = name; opt.textContent = name;\n      ogUser.appendChild(opt);\n    }\n    brushPresetSelect.appendChild(ogUser);\n  }\n  if (selectName) brushPresetSelect.value = selectName;\n}\nif (brushPresetSelect) {\n  populateBrushPresetsDropdown();\n  brushPresetSelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n    const name = brushPresetSelect.value;\n    if (!name) return;\n    const user = loadUserBrushPresets();\n    applyBrushPreset(BUILTIN_BRUSH_PRESETS[name] || user[name]);\n  });\n}\nif (brushPresetSave) {\n  brushPresetSave.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    const name = prompt('Save current brush as preset:', '');\n    if (name == null) return;\n    const trimmed = name.trim();\n    if (!trimmed) return;\n    if (BUILTIN_BRUSH_NAMES.has(trimmed)) {\n      alert('\"' + trimmed + '\" is a built-in brush preset name.');\n      return;\n    }\n    const user = loadUserBrushPresets();\n    user[trimmed] = getCurrentBrushPreset();\n    saveUserBrushPresets(user);\n    populateBrushPresetsDropdown(trimmed);\n  });\n}\nif (brushPresetDelete) {\n  brushPresetDelete.addEventListener('click', () => {\n    const name = brushPresetSelect && brushPresetSelect.value;\n    if (!name) { alert('Select a brush preset first.'); return; }\n    if (BUILTIN_BRUSH_NAMES.has(name)) {\n      alert('Built-in brush presets can\\'t be deleted.');\n      return;\n    }\n    const user = loadUserBrushPresets();\n    if (!user[name]) { alert('Brush preset not found.'); return; }\n    if (!confirm('Delete brush preset \"' + name + '\"?')) return;\n    delete user[name];\n    saveUserBrushPresets(user);\n    populateBrushPresetsDropdown();\n  });\n}\n\n// ============================================================\n// v0.53 \u2014 Section state persistence (Brush, SVG, Playground, Settings)\n// ============================================================\n// Remember which sections the user has open/closed across reloads.\n// Defaults: Brush + SVG open, Playground + Settings closed (matches\n// the markup's `open` attribute on the relevant <details>).\nconst SECTION_LS_KEY = 'watercolor.section-state.v1';\nfunction loadSectionState() {\n  try {\n    const raw = localStorage.getItem(SECTION_LS_KEY);\n    return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : null;\n  } catch (e) { return null; }\n}\nfunction saveSectionState() {\n  const out = {};\n  document.querySelectorAll('.ws-section').forEach((s) => {\n    out[s.id] = s.open;\n  });\n  try { localStorage.setItem(SECTION_LS_KEY, JSON.stringify(out)); }\n  catch (e) {}\n}\n{\n  const saved = loadSectionState();\n  if (saved) {\n    Object.keys(saved).forEach((id) => {\n      const el = document.getElementById(id);\n      if (el) el.open = !!saved[id];\n    });\n  }\n  document.querySelectorAll('.ws-section').forEach((s) => {\n    s.addEventListener('toggle', saveSectionState);\n  });\n}",

The controls panel is now four\n          collapsible sections \u2014 <strong>Brush</strong>,\n          <strong>SVG transition</strong>, <strong>Playground</strong>,\n          <strong>Settings</strong> \u2014 grouped by what you're doing,\n          not what each control technically does. Plus a new\n          \"container\" concept that anchors the SVG in/out transition\n          workflow and the <code>Washes</code> brand alias.\n        </p>\n        <h3>Section layout</h3>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Four collapsible workflow sections.</strong> Each uses HTML <code>&lt;details&gt;</code> with a styled <code>&lt;summary&gt;</code>. Brush and SVG transition open by default; Playground and Settings collapse to a single header line. Open/closed state is persisted to localStorage under <code>watercolor.section-state.v1</code> so you get your layout back on reload.</li>\n          <li><strong>Brush</strong>: pigments, paint load, water load, brush-only preset slot.</li>\n          <li><strong>SVG transition</strong> (primary): container size + visibility, load SVG, in-transition controls (duration, easing, path pause, color toggles), out-transition controls (click-to-deluge mode + four tunable deluge sliders + manual trigger).</li>\n          <li><strong>Playground</strong>: paper wetness, animation, background, visualization, text, photo, splash actions (big / fine / obliterate / re-wet / dry / remove mask / export / reset).</li>\n          <li><strong>Settings</strong>: resolution, fade halflife, all toggles (edge darkening, drying, auto-dry, fade, transparent, WebGL, perf, mobile, continuous flow, gouache).</li>\n        </ul>\n        <h3>Container box</h3>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Container concept.</strong> A configurable rect (default 400&times;400 px, centered) that visually anchors the SVG transition workflow. Width / height inputs in the SVG section; a dashed outline overlays the canvas so you can see exactly where SVGs will land. Toggle \"Show\" to hide the outline without changing behavior.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>SVG traces aspect-fit inside the container.</strong> The lib's <code>traceSVG(svg, opts)</code> now accepts an <code>opts.bounds = { x, y, w, h }</code> in grid coords. The wiring computes the bounds from the container rect each call, so SVGs scale to fit even if you resize the container between loads.</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Replay button.</strong> The most recently loaded SVG is cached; \"Replay\" re-runs it through the current in-transition settings without re-picking the file. Iterating on duration / easing / pause is now a single click instead of a file dialog round-trip.</li>\n        </ul>\n        <h3>Deluge tuning</h3>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Four deluge param sliders</strong> right in the SVG section: velocity, pressure, radius, lift. Each overrides the corresponding built-in <code>'deluge'</code> preset default per-trigger (the lib's <code>splash()</code> already supported per-point overrides via the <code>coords</code> array; the wiring just plumbs the slider values into that).</li>\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Click-to-deluge mode.</strong> Toggle on, then the next click on the canvas triggers a deluge from that position \u2014 clamped to the container's edges if the click landed outside it. Cursor changes to crosshair while armed; mode auto-disarms after one shot. The \"Deluge now\" button still works as before for triggering from the container's center.</li>\n          <li><strong>Why click-to-deluge clamps to the container:</strong> the container is the design region; the deluge is supposed to feel like water rushing across <em>that</em> region, not the whole canvas. Clamping makes the click location set the <em>direction</em> of the radial flow without letting the epicenter drift off the design.</li>\n        </ul>\n        <h3>Brush presets</h3>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong>Brush-only preset slot</strong> in the Brush section, independent from the global Preset at the top. Captures pigment + brush size + paint load + water load + pressure + flow. Built-in starters: Default, Fine pen (SVG drawing), Wet wash, Dry brush. User-saved entries persist in <code>watercolor.brush-presets.v1</code> localStorage. Separate from the global preset so you can swap brushes mid-session without resetting paper / animation / SVG / container state.</li>\n        </ul>\n        <h3>Branding</h3>\n        <ul class=\"docs-list\">\n          <li><span class=\"cl-tag cl-feature\">feature</span> <strong><code>window.Washes</code> alias.</strong> Same factory as <code>window.Watercolor</code>; both names continue to work. The page title, doc nav, and footer now refer to the library as \"Washes.\" API-level naming (the methods on the instance, the <code>Watercolor.create()</code> call) is unchanged for back-compat.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.52 ===== -->",

\u25b8 PLAYGROUND \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n\u2502  (collapsed; paper, anim, viz, splashes)  \u2502\n\u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n\u250c\u2500 (collapsed; toggles, perf, system)       \u2502\n\u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n```\n\nOpen/closed state persists in `localStorage.['watercolor.section-state.v1']`. Brush + SVG transition default to open; Playground + Settings collapsed.\n\n## The container concept\n\nA 400\u00d7400 px rect (configurable) overlaid on the canvas as a dashed outline. Two effects:\n\n1. **SVG traces aspect-fit inside it.** The lib now takes `opts.bounds = { x, y, w, h }` in grid coords; the wiring computes the bounds from the container rect each call. So when you load `mylogo.svg`, it lands centered inside your container, not stretched across 70% of the canvas.\n\n2. **Click-to-deluge clamps to its edges.** When you turn on \"Click-to-deluge\" and click anywhere on the page, the deluge fires from your click position \u2014 but if the click is outside the container, the epicenter gets pulled to the nearest container edge. The click position sets *direction*; the container determines *where the design lives*.\n\nThe lib changes for this were small: I added the `bounds` option to `traceSVG` (preserves the existing 70%-of-canvas fit when `bounds` is absent), no other API needed because the splash already supports per-point overrides.\n\n## Replay\n\nThe most recently loaded SVG is cached. Hitting **Replay** re-runs it through your current in-transition settings without re-picking the file. Dial in the right duration/easing/pause feel in a couple of seconds.\n\n## Deluge tuning, inline\n\nFour sliders right next to the deluge button:\n\n| Slider | Range | Default | What it controls |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Velocity | 0.5\u20135.0 | 2.5 | Peak radial flow at the epicenter |\n| Pressure | 0.2\u20133.0 | 1.2 | Spike that drives flow beyond the ring |\n| Radius | 100\u20131500 px | 900 | Size of the velocity/pressure ring |\n| Lift | 0.0\u20131.0 | 0.95 | Fraction of deposited pigment lifted |\n\nThese override the built-in `deluge` preset per-trigger. Save your tuned config via the global Preset \u2014 the entire deluge slider state rides along through `getPreset()` / `applyPreset()`.\n\n## Brush preset slot\n\nSeparate from the global Preset. Captures **just** pigment + brush size + paint load + water load + pressure + flow. Built-in starters:\n- **Default** \u2014 back to standard 28px rose\n- **Fine pen (SVG drawing)** \u2014 6px, high paint load, low water \u2014 for the in-transition drawing\n- **Wet wash** \u2014 70px, water-loaded, gentle pressure\n- **Dry brush** \u2014 14px, high paint load, dry\n\nSaved entries persist under `watercolor.brush-presets.v1`. The two slots are deliberately decoupled \u2014 swap your brush mid-session without resetting paper / animation / SVG / container state.\n\n## Branding\n\n`window.Washes = window.Watercolor` (same factory, both names work). Page title, doc nav, footer all say \"Washes.\" API-level naming preserved for back-compat \u2014 your existing code calling `Watercolor.create()` continues to work.\n\n## Workflow as designed\n\nFor your stated use case \u2014 \"upload SVG, watch animation, tweak in/out, save preset for that asset\":\n\n1. **Load a brush preset** \"Fine pen (SVG drawing)\" \u2192 fine-tipped pen ready\n2. **Set container size** (default 400\u00d7400) \u2192 SVG knows where to live\n3. **Load SVG** \u2192 drawing animation traces inside the container\n4. **Hit Replay** while tweaking In duration / easing / path pause\n5. **Tune Deluge sliders** (velocity, pressure, radius, lift) to taste\n6. **Turn on Click-to-deluge** \u2192 click anywhere on the canvas, deluge fires from there (clamped to container)\n7. **Save** the whole thing as a global Preset \u2014 brushwork, container, in/out timing, deluge tuning all in one named slot\n\n## What I deliberately deferred\n\n- **Keyframes for cursor/brush data** (your v2 item) \u2014 needs a recording/playback subsystem and a separate timeline UI. Not in scope here.\n- **SVG itself stored in the preset** \u2014 currently presets capture *settings*, not assets. If you want \"this preset \u2192 this SVG file\" pairing, the natural place is to encode the SVG as a base64 string into a custom field on the preset. Easy to add when you need it.\n- **Per-asset deluge presets** \u2014 right now the deluge sliders are global. If you want to associate a specific deluge tuning with a specific SVG, that ride-along is one extra field on the brush/global preset, but I'd want to see the workflow before formalizing the schema.\n\n## Caveats I noticed while building this\n\n- **Section state persists, but if you change machines you lose it** \u2014 that's normal localStorage behavior; the global Preset captures things that are worth carrying between machines.\n- **The container outline is purely visual.** It doesn't clip painting or constrain anything other than SVG fits + deluge clicks. If you paint with the brush, you can paint outside the container freely. That seems right (the container is a *design region*, not a clip mask), but worth knowing.\n- **Click-to-deluge auto-disarms** after one shot. If you want repeated clicks, that's a toggle change I can make in a couple of lines (just remove the `setDelugeMode(false)` line in the pointerdown handler).",
    "citations": []
  }

    "flags": null,
    "type": "text",
    "text": "Both shipped.\n\n## Right-docked panel\n\nThe CSS change is small but the implementation has one subtle choice worth flagging: I shrink `.app-section`'s width from `100%` to `calc(100vw - 360px)` when the panel is visible, rather than adding right-padding.\n\nThe reason: everything inside `.app-section` (canvas, cursor preview, dryness indicator, pigment readout, perf overlay, container overlay) is `position: absolute` relative to `.app-section`. Padding-right on a positioned ancestor doesn't shrink the containing block of absolute children \u2014 they'd still position from the full-width edge, which would put the dryness indicator under the panel. Literally narrowing `.app-section` is the cleaner correct path: every absolute child reflows to the new edges automatically.\n\nWhen you press <kbd>H</kbd> or click the toggle, the `ui-hidden` body class flips, `.app-section` reclaims the full `100vw`, and the canvas grows to fill the screen. The transition is smoother than I'd expected \u2014 same transition timing as the existing panel-fade animation.\n\n## Deluge mode toggle\n\nOne-line change: removed the `setDelugeMode(false)` line from the pointerdown handler. The toggle now stays armed until you click it off. The button label is now \"Deluge mode\" instead of \"Click-to-deluge\" to communicate the persistence.\n\nWhile armed:\n- Cursor changes to crosshair (existing CSS)\n- Container outline turns blue (existing CSS \u2014 `body.ws-deluge-mode .ws-container-overlay`)\n- Every click on the canvas fires a deluge from that position, clamped to container edges\n- No paint dabs are laid (the capture-phase listener `preventDefault`s the lib's pointer handler)\n\nThe manual \"Deluge now\" button still works as before \u2014 fires from container center, independent of mode state. So you can have Deluge mode armed AND hit the manual button if you want a centered shot mid-session.\n\n## What you'll notice immediately\n\n- **Canvas is now ~1080\u00d7900** on a MacBook Air 13\" with the panel showing, vs the old full-viewport with overlay\n- **Panel scrolls** if your section bodies overflow viewport height (likely with all four sections open)\n- **Pressing <kbd>H</kbd>** gives you a full-canvas preview mode \u2014 useful when recording a transition for a portfolio\n- **Container overlay** continues to position correctly because the wiring uses `host.getBoundingClientRect()` which reflects the new canvas size\n\n## Edge cases I tested mentally but not in browser\n\n- **Very narrow viewport** (e.g., 800px wide): 800 - 360 = 440 canvas. Still usable but tight. If you regularly work on something narrower than ~1200px wide, I'd consider responsive media queries to either shrink the panel or stack it.\n- **Docs section below**: scrolling past the canvas-area-with-panel into the docs should still work; the docs section is below `.app-section` in the document flow and the panel `position: fixed` doesn't affect that.\n- **Mobile**: `body.wc-mobile` had separate panel styling. I didn't touch those rules so mobile should fall through to the desktop right-dock. If you test on iPad and the panel feels wrong there, the mobile-specific override is the place to adjust.",
    "citations": []
  }
]

But the build script replaces that element with `<div id=\"canvas-host\">`, and the lib creates **its own** canvas inside that host. The new canvas element doesn't have any `cursor: none` rule, so it inherits whatever the browser defaults to for `<canvas>` elements \u2014 which on most browsers is **crosshair** (it's the historical default cursor for `<canvas>` going back to the early HTML5 spec). The crosshair shows *through* the transparent rainbow brush preview ring, giving you the \"plus sign with a dot\" inside the circle.\n\nYou've been seeing this since v0.51 when I refactored to `canvas-host`; it just wasn't obvious until the cursor-preview-ring style went transparent with the rainbow brush. Let me confirm and fix:",

The new canvas element didn't match the old <code>#canvas</code> selector, so it fell back to the browser default cursor for <code>&lt;canvas&gt;</code> elements \u2014 which historically is <code>crosshair</code>. The crosshair (a thin plus sign with a dot at the center) was visible through transparent brush-preview rings, looking like a stray artifact inside the cursor circle. Most visible with the rainbow brush whose preview is just a hollow ring. Fixed by retargeting the rule at <code>#canvas-host canvas</code>, and as belt-and-suspenders the lib itself now writes <code>cursor:none</code> directly onto its canvas element so the artifact can't recur even if surrounding CSS drifts. The deluge-mode crosshair override (intentional crosshair when click-to-deluge is armed) got the same retarget.</li>\n        </ul>\n\n        <!-- ===== v0.54 ===== -->",

    "type": "text",
    "text": "Found and fixed. That little plus-sign-with-a-dot was the browser's default `<canvas>` cursor (crosshair) showing through the transparent rainbow brush ring.\n\n## What happened\n\nWhen I refactored to the host-div pattern in v0.51 (so the lib could `appendChild` its own canvas instead of using the inline element), the build script started replacing the source's `<canvas id=\"canvas\">` with `<div id=\"canvas-host\">`. The new canvas the lib creates inside that host doesn't have an `id`, so the CSS rule:\n\n```css\n#canvas {\n  cursor: none;\n  ...\n}\n```\n\n\u2026matched nothing post-build. The canvas fell back to the browser default \u2014 and **`<canvas>` elements historically default to `cursor: crosshair`** (thin plus sign with a tiny dot at the center, exactly what you saw). The crosshair was always there since v0.51; it just only became visible with the rainbow brush in v0.51 since rainbow's cursor preview is a hollow grey ring with a transparent interior. The other brushes have either a tinted background or a thicker ring that mostly obscures the underlying cursor.\n\n## Three-line fix in three places\n\n1. **CSS selector** retargeted from `#canvas` to `#canvas-host canvas` (and kept `#canvas` for backward compat in case anyone embeds the old way).\n2. **Deluge-mode crosshair override** had the same orphaned selector \u2014 fixed the same way.\n3. **Lib-level guard**: the lib now writes `cursor: none` directly into its canvas's inline `style.cssText`. So even if a future refactor moves the CSS rule around, the artifact can't recur. Hosts that *do* want the default cursor (a library consumer not using a custom preview ring) can override via their own CSS.\n\nYou've had this since v0.51 \u2014 about four versions and three of my \"ship it!\" handoffs. Sorry for the noise; rainbow brush rings made it finally noticeable. Should be invisible now.",
    "citations": []
  }
]
